title: Top-tier market validation
detail: 106,772 stars and 6,908 forks. AGPL-3.0 with commercial hosted tier at firecrawl.dev. TypeScript 68.2%. Active v2 development.

title: Engine-fallback scraping abstraction
detail: scrapeURL directory houses 8 pluggable engines (fetch, fire-engine, playwright, pdf, document, wikipedia, index, utils) coordinated by buildFallbackList + scrapeURLWithEngine + named NoEnginesLeftError. Mermaid diagram in README documents signal flow.

title: 13-sub-app monorepo with polyglot SDKs
detail: apps/ contains api, ui, 7 language SDKs (js, python, rust, go, java, elixir, playwright-service-ts), plus nuq-postgres, redis, test-site, test-suite sub-apps. Wire contract enforced by 3 frozen OpenAPI specs.

title: Version-parallel API trees (v0/v1/v2)
detail: Three parallel controller directories with their own test fixtures (snips/v0, snips/v1, snips/v2) and three frozen OpenAPI specs (openapi-v0.json, v1-openapi.json, openapi.json). New features land on v2; v0 kept for backward compat.

title: Two queue systems coexisting without documented migration
detail: BullMQ (Redis-backed) and nuq-postgres (custom Postgres queue) both active. Seven worker types total (queue-worker, extract-worker, index-worker, nuq-worker, nuq-prefetch-worker, nuq-reconciler-worker, etc.). Migration plan or hybrid rationale not documented.

title: Credit-metered auth with distributed locking
detail: Auth model: API key → Supabase lookup → Redis-cached ACUC (Auth Credit Usage Chunk) → Redlock-serialized cache updates. Well beyond typical bearer-check. Tests passing for rate-limiter.

title: Zod-at-the-boundary validation
detail: URL validation duty moved from in-code helper (WebScraperDataProvider.validateInitialUrl) to Zod layer above scrapeUrl. Clean 'validate at boundary, trust internally' pattern. Zod 4.1.12.

title: Exemplary test stratification
detail: 9 parallel E2E buckets (snips/v0/v1/v2, e2e_noAuth, e2e_withAuth, e2e_full_withAuth, e2e_v1_withAuth, e2e_v1_withAuth_all_params, e2e_extract, e2e_map, deep-research/unit). Five Jest invocations (test, test:local-no-auth, test:full, test:prod, test:snips) targeting different environments.

title: Capability-gated test pattern documented in CLAUDE.md
detail: Tests gate on runtime env vars (TEST_SUITE_SELF_HOSTED, OPENAI_API_KEY, OLLAMA_BASE_URL) rather than CI fences. Same file runs in all environments; gate decides whether assertions execute. 3 lines per gate.

title: Harness-as-boot-orchestrator
detail: pnpm harness jest boots API + workers before tests. CLAUDE.md explicitly warns against pnpm start. Dedicated test-site sub-app provides deterministic scrape target.

title: Zero architecture prose docs
detail: No docs/ directory. One mermaid diagram (scrapeURL README) is the only architecture doc. Queue layout, auth flow, v0→v1→v2 migration paths, service boundaries — all have to be inferred from code + CLAUDE.md (19 lines).

title: 58-app examples cookbook
detail: examples/ has 58 self-contained apps pairing firecrawl with specific LLMs (Claude 3.7, GPT-4.1, Gemini 2.5, Llama 4, Mistral, DeepSeek R1, o1/o3/o4-mini, Grok, Groq) or frameworks (openai_swarm, e2b, kubernetes). Each ~50-150 lines. Functions as the de facto cookbook.

title: Exemplary minimal CLAUDE.md
detail: 19 lines of actionable AI instructions: test-first, E2E preferred (called 'snips'), capability gating, mandatory scrapeTimeout helper, harness usage. Higher signal density than most 200-line CLAUDE.md files. Direct comparison candidate for SoNash's 235-line CLAUDE.md.

title: Production-posture service layer
detail: 14+ dedicated service subdirectories: alerts, autumn (billing), billing, idempotency, indexing, ledger, logging, notification, webhook, subscription, system-monitor. Sentry integration with sourcemap injection. Indicates a mature operational culture.

title: Manual polyglot SDK parity
detail: 7 language SDKs manually re-implement the wire contract. Parity enforced via 3 frozen OpenAPI specs + test-suite app. Adding a new endpoint is a 7-file change. No shared package or code-gen step.

title: LLM-ready output as first-class feature
detail: PDFs routed directly to Markdown via LlamaParse (not through plaintext). DOCXs routed directly to HTML → Markdown via mammoth. scrapeURL README documents this evolution from the older WebScraperDataProvider. LLM-consumable output is the product, not a postprocessing step.

title: No visible sanitization / CSP / secret-scan layer
detail: Didn't find a top-level HTML sanitizer wrapper, CSP middleware, or secret-scanning config in the quick sweep of apps/api. Relies on Zod at boundary + downstream markdown converter. Coverage audit should verify.

title: Harness-mandated test discipline
detail: CLAUDE.md mandates: write E2E tests FIRST, use 'pnpm harness jest' (not manual pnpm start), always use scrapeTimeout helper, gate tests by capability. This is enforced discipline captured in AI instructions.

title: LLM examples kept current
detail: examples/ contains variants for every current major model: Claude 3.7, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.5, Gemini 2.0/2.5, Llama 4 Maverick, Mistral-small-3.1, DeepSeek R1, DeepSeek V3, o1, o3, o3-mini, o4-mini, Grok, Groq. Pattern of tracking model releases with named example apps.

title: Deep Research + Extract as sub-products
detail: apps/api/src/lib/deep-research/ and apps/api/src/lib/extract/ are full libraries with their own test trees (deep-research has __tests__/unit/). These are essentially sub-products within the API — each is a named v2 endpoint.
